lines: Carter is having an absolute field day — Algeria drew 3-3, England battered Panama, DR Congo hammered Uzbekistan, and Colombia held Portugal. Four teams, all still breathing. The smug cunt probably printed a spreadsheet.
Ellis's Argentina are cruising at 9 points and top of Group J, which is great, except she's also dragging around Paraguay and New Zealand like two dead cats tied to her ankles.
Croft, mate — Uzbekistan are officially out, Uruguay are sitting on 2 points with a loss, and France are the only thing keeping you relevant. You are essentially a passenger on someone else's bus.

Morocco are still going strong in Group C, which means Butters, Callum, Jim and Wilmot are all riding that particular camel — four cunts, one team, and only one of them's going to feel good about it eventually.

Today's Round of 32 action kicks off with 2A vs 2B, which depending on how the groups shake out could drag several of our lot into knockout jeopardy — watch the Group B and Group D runners-up spots closely, because Dene's Bosnia and The Foreman's Türkiye are both sweating on the edge of the sofa right now.
